cases.title: Broker Bankruptcy & Agency Liability — Unauthorized Dwelling Case
cases.sourceReference: Zoning verification & agency liability — triplex / municipal compliance
cases.summary: Broker failed to verify zoning compliance of a triplex. The third dwelling was unauthorized. Broker later declared bankruptcy. Court held the agency liable.
cases.facts: Property sold as triplex based on leases, tax bills, and certificate of location. No verification with municipality. City later confirmed third dwelling illegal.
cases.legalIssues: 1. Broker duty to verify zoning compliance. 2. Agency liability. 3. Effect of broker bankruptcy.
cases.decision: Broker committed a fault. Agency liable. Broker protected by bankruptcy stay.
cases.reasoning: A prudent broker would have verified with the municipality. Documents relied upon did not confirm legality. Bankruptcy does not eliminate liability but prevents enforcement.

rules.title: Broker must verify zoning/legal use
rules.description: Broker must verify legal compliance of property use with municipality when marketing number of dwellings.

rules.title: Leases and tax bills are insufficient proof
rules.description: Leases, tax accounts, and certificates of location do not confirm zoning compliance.

rules.title: Agency liable for broker faults
rules.description: Agency is liable for faults committed by brokers under its supervision.

rules.title: Bankruptcy stays enforcement only
rules.description: Broker bankruptcy prevents enforcement but does not eliminate liability.
